mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 7.0%, it signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recession, forcing investors to price in negative earnings revisions that drive S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 7.0%, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with a falling federal funds rate indicates a steepening yield curve driven by strong credit demand, which predicts positive equity returns as corporate borrowing costs remain manageable despite economic expansion.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, triggering short-term momentum buying that pushes S&P 500 returns positive over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a directional hit rate below 45% across all post-shock windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which mechanically supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over 5-year windows, or payroll growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index crosses above 225.0, it indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 225.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for Fed policy expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and a rise in the unemployment rate signals a synchronized downturn in interest-sensitive sectors and labor demand, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as recession risk pricing accelerates.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms fundamental economic expansion, leading to earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery phases, with a predictive accuracy below 40% across all post-shock windows.
